body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: unknown; driver_capability: ready

## Scenario

Scenario-meanings `shell-escape-command`: the agent runs a shell command outside the formal tool framework; irrlicht stays `working` during execution and resolves to `ready` when the command exits. User-observable signal: `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`, no stick in `working`.

## agent_supports: yes

Codex 0.130.0's TUI has a user-typed shell escape. The binary string is literal: "Prefix a command with `!` to run it locally. Example: `!ls`" (strings scan of the darwin-arm64 vendor binary). Typed empirically in a tmux probe: `!ls` renders "â€˘ You ran ls" and `!sleep 5` renders "â€˘ Running sleep 5" then "â€˘ Working (1s â€˘ esc to interrupt)" for the full 5 s, then "â€˘ You ran sleep 5". So the TUI itself holds the session in a working/interruptible state for the entire command runtime and returns to the prompt when it exits â€” the exact `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` shape the scenario describes, with no LLM round-trip.

This is a genuinely distinct affordance from `full-lifecycle-toolcall`: that scenario exercises the model's `exec`/`function_call` tool (assistant decides to run a command); this one is a USER-initiated `!`-prefix shell escape with no model turn. The rollout proves the difference â€” a `!`-escape writes NO `function_call`/`function_call_output`, only a `task_started`, a synthetic `user_message` (role=user) whose content is a `<user_shell_command><command>â€¦</command><result>Exit code: 0â€¦</result></user_shell_command>` block, and a `task_complete`.

## daemon_capability: unknown; driver_capability: ready

The daemon (FilesUnderRoot watch on `~/.codex/sessions`, codex parser) DOES observe a `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` arc for a `!`-escape, with no stuck-working and no premature-ready â€” the load-bearing intent of the scenario. Confirmed against the live recording daemon (pid 98544) for the probe session: two clean `state_transition` pairs (readyâ†’working `force readyâ†’working on first activity`, workingâ†’ready `agent finished turn â†’ ready`), one per `!`-escape, and a terminal `process_exited`.

The gap that makes this `partial` rather than `yes`: the working DWELL the daemon records does NOT cover the command's actual runtime. Codex writes `task_started` to the rollout the instant the command begins, but the codex parser skips `task_started` (parser.go:155-172 emits only `task_complete` as `turn_done`); the only activity the parser turns into a state change is the synthetic `user_message`, which codex does not flush until the command FINISHES. For `!sleep 5` the rollout showed `task_started` at T+0, then `user_message`+`task_complete` together at T+5s. The daemon therefore transitioned readyâ†’working only at command-completion time and settled back to ready ~2 s later (its debounce window), so the recorded working arc is debounce-bounded (~2 s) rather than command-bounded (~5 s). Topology and final state are correct and observable; the literal "stays working FOR THE DURATION" is not, because the parser has no event to anchor the working start to until the command is already done.

## Capability anchor

Primitive exercised = shell-escape mechanism (Claude Code `!cmd`, or analogous). Codex's analogue is the TUI `!`-prefix, anchored to `capabilities.json -> features.tui: true` (the escape is a TUI-only affordance; codex `exec` headless has no interactive `!` shell mode).
caveats: Working dwell is debounce-bounded (~2 s), not command-bounded: the codex parser skips task_started, and codex only flushes the synthetic user_message when the command exits, so the daemon cannot anchor the working-start to the command-start. Topology (readyâ†’workingâ†’ready) and final state are correct; the literal 'stays working for the whole command' is not directly observable. This is the feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant pattern applied to a timing detail.
The !-escape is TUI-only; codex exec (headless) has no interactive ! shell mode. The scenario's requires:[headless_mode] is a scenario-level field; this cell is recorded interactively via tmux.

Unlike claudecode (whose parser deliberately skips <bash-input>/<bash-stdout> so state stays ready throughout), codex's !-escape DOES synthesize a turn (task_started/user_message/task_complete) and DOES drive a readyâ†’workingâ†’ready arc.

sources.note: event_msg/task_complete â†’ turn_done; task_started and other event_msg payloads skipped (lines 155-172); user/developer message â†’ user_message with ClearToolNames
rule 4 default 'transcript activity â†’ working'; rule 2b 'agent finished turn â†’ ready' on turn_done â€” the two transitions observed for the probe
features.tui: true â€” the capability anchor for the !-prefix shell escape
strings scan: 'Prefix a command with ! to run it locally. Example: !ls'
!ls => task_started/user_message(<user_shell_command>)/task_complete same-ms; !sleep 5 => task_started at T+0, user_message+task_complete at T+5s, no function_call
